qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Legacy invitation acceptance lets a member claim any pending invite by supplying arbitrary inviterId
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a legacy invitation-acceptance authorization flaw in invitation/user-management handlers, while GHSA-gfvg-qv54-r4pc is an authenticated arbitrary file-read issue caused by improper file access controls in filesystem-oriented workflow nodes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only qualifying finding concerns missing inviter-to-invitee binding checks in the legacy invitation acceptance flow, not improper file access controls that allow authenticated arbitrary host file reads.
evidence: GHSA-gfvg-qv54-r4pc describes authenticated arbitrary file read from the n8n host and names filesystem-related nodes such as "Read/Write Files from Disk" and "Git" as the affected surface.

The only qualifying finding points to `packages/cli/src/controllers/invitation.controller.ts` and `packages/cli/src/services/user.service.ts`, where invitation acceptance trusts attacker-supplied inviter and invitee identifiers.

The qualifying finding's exploit path is member enumeration plus `POST /rest/invitations/:id/accept` account takeover; it does not involve a file-read sink, filesystem node, or the advisory's file access control mechanism.
